subject: Product Management
title: The Rise of Product Ops
description: Learn The Rise of Product Ops in one minute.

hook.text: Product Ops is the newest team in product organizations — and it's growing faster than anyone expected.

buildup.text: Product Ops handles the operational side: tooling, data infrastructure, process optimization, and user research ops.

discovery.text: By offloading operational work, Product Ops frees PMs to spend more time on strategy and customer discovery.

twist.text: Critics call it unnecessary bureaucracy. Advocates say it's the only way PM teams can scale without burning out.

climax.text: Companies like Uber, Airbnb, and Stripe have built dedicated Product Ops teams — a sign of where the field is heading.

punchline.text: Product Ops: the team that makes PM teams work.

quiz.question: What does a Product Ops team do?
options: Handles operational tasks like tooling and data, freeing PMs for strategy
